source.title: Emotional regulation of interactions between two-month-olds and their mothers

confidence_rationale: The double-video replay paradigm isolates temporal contingency from affective content: infants watched either a live video feed of their mother (contingent) or a replay of the same mother's behavior from minutes earlier (same face, same vocalizations, identical content but time-shifted, non-contingent). Two-month-olds showed reduced positive affect and gaze during replay despite identical visual content. This dissociates contingency detection from content recognition, showing that the monitoring system tracks the time-relationship of responses, not just their presence. This is the clearest evidence that temporal contingency monitoring is operational at 2 months. Replicated by Stormark and Braarud (2004) with the same double-video method.
failure_signatures: Original study had small N (four 2-month-olds in early version); later replications have larger samples.
The closed-circuit TV setup introduces some ecological artificiality versus face-to-face interaction.
Some replication attempts found the effect smaller or less consistent than the original, raising questions about the exact temporal window of sensitivity.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Two-month-old infants distinguish live (contingent) from time-delayed replay (non-contingent) maternal interaction via closed-circuit TV, showing significantly more positive affect and gaze in the contingent condition despite identical facial/vocal content.
mapping.ree_translation: Directly isolates the monitoring signal from content. The infant system is comparing the temporal structure of incoming responses against an expected contingency template, not merely reacting to presence/absence of social stimulation. For Q-035: this is strong evidence that the monitoring architecture operates continuously on a millisecond-to-second timescale from 2 months -- before any theory-of-mind, before pretend play, before language. The content being tracked at 2 months is extremely simple (is this partner responding to what I just did?) but the temporal monitoring mechanism is the same architecture that will later track rule compliance and strategy fairness.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The double-video paradigm tests sensitivity to temporal displacement of a familiar partner's responses, not sensitivity to mid-game content changes. It isolates the timing channel of monitoring but not the semantic channel. The REE play frame may need to monitor both.
mapping.source_context: Closed-circuit TV setup, mother-infant dyads, 2-month-olds, Edinburgh/Cambridge labs. Later replicated by Nadel et al. (1999), Stormark and Braarud (2004).

confidence_components.notes: Classic paradigm with multiple replications. Source quality high despite pre-registration era. The double-video isolation of contingency from content is a methodological strength that directly speaks to the REE monitoring architecture question.
